title: Too many preprocessor tokens on a line (without any `define in the program)
body:
The attached file is generated by a compiler. The original file is >200,000 lines of system verilog in the same `module`. I've whittled it down to a single assignment that fails.

### Reproduction

Test file: [out.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/5882037/out.txt)

```
verilator --lint-only out.txt
```

**Expected**: The file is parsed correctly and reports warnings.
**Actual**: Verilator says:
```
%Error: out.sv:7:57629: Too many preprocessor tokens on a line (>20000); perhaps recursive `define
%Error: out.sv:7:57628: syntax error, unexpected $end, expecting TYPE-IDENTIFIER
%Error: Cannot continue
```

**Note**: There are NO defines in the input file.

**Verilator version**: Verilator 4.108 2021-01-10 rev v4.106-158-g484b76e5b

### Yosys test

I also tried to parse the file with `yosys` which works correctly:
```
yosys -p 'read_verilog -sv out.sv
```

### Workarounds

Since this is generated code, I'm also willing to try out workarounds (for example, changing the `assign` in some manner).

**Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?**
Depending on the amount of work represented.
